channel.title: Favorites on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Favorites on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: My Favorite Shipped Designs
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my favorite shipped designs.

It’s a fun Friday. I want to answer a question from ToastManDennis1218, who asked: could you please make the same format video with your ideas that were accepted, made it into your games and you are happy they did? This was on my video of my ideas that got rejected, which I link below. By the way, this video has a lot of videos in it, and I’ll link them all below. Um, I won’t always be able to give all the context. This might be a long video. I won’t always be able to give all the context for all the design ideas I’ve worked on, but there’s a ton of videos associated with this video that will give you a lot of background on them. So before I dive into my list, though, of things I designed that went into my game that I really liked, I’m going to limit it in two ways, because what would be a Tim?

item.title: Favorite Chocolate
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk to you about my favorite chocolate.

I did a chocolate video a few weeks ago. It was very well received. Um, a lot of people asked me — because my chocolate Blog has over 2,000 entries — what my favorite chocolates were. I’m happy to talk about chocolate and it’s Friday, so let’s do a fun Friday video. I’m going to start with my absolute favorite chocolate bar — just bar none. Then I’m going to talk about what I think the best brands are. I tried to make a top five but there was a four-way tie for the fifth slot.

item.title: My Top Five Video Games
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about my top five video games ever.

I’ve been thinking about putting this list together for months, but I was never quite sure which ones to include, and then I realized I really had to go with the ones that were really influential for me and were also maybe the first of its type that I ever saw, the first, either one of its genre or one with that feature, and not I was originally putting in the games I had played the most, I had the most hours in, but I decided to go with ones that I thought were the most influential and the ones I really remember, and go back to um, the. In this list, however, there is not a single game that is newer than 24 years old, and one of them is 44 years old, so strap in the first one. I’m going to go chronologically, by the way, so these are my top five, but I’m not going to. Tell you what the order is to. Going to tell you what the order is, this is chronological.

item.title: My Favorite Sci Fi Novel
item.description:
Hi, it’s Tim. I’m back with the answer to the first topic that, uh, someone had a question about on my last video, and that was user Hyperion said that

I had mentioned that Walter Miller’s book I can’t go of Leibowitz was really influential on the design of Fallout setting asked what other sci-fi books I liked — um, of course I have a lot of books. Um, this is just a fraction of them. Uh, growing up in the 70s, obviously, Arthur C Clarke, Robert Heinlein, Isaac Asimov, Ursula the Gwynne were just hugely influential. When I got older, though, as a young man I guess I read more of Dan Simmons Hyperion series, Ian Banks culture novels, Rogers zelazny’s Amber novels — um, those are the kind of books I really liked to read. But I do have one absolute Freight favorite book of all time, and that would be Lord of Light by Roger zlosny. I love this book, I love it, I love it has everything I want in a book. It’s well written. It’s non-linear, it incorporates. It kind of reads as a fantasy book, but then it turns into a Sci-Fi novel, incorporates religion into the story in a really seamless way. It very gradually reveals what the world is, which I really like. I don’t like front loading everything on you. Um, I love this book so much that I not only own — this is my science fiction book club — but I’ve caught an early edition and I have the Easton press leather bound copy. That’s the only book I think I own multiple copies of.
